¡Para MAF Chile, importante empresa de Financiamiento Automotriz buscamos Analista de Riesgo Portafolio!🎯 Objetivo del cargoMonitorear y analizar el comportamiento del portafolio con el fin de anticipar deterioros, proponer acciones de mitigación y resguardar la estabilidad y rentabilidad del negocio. El rol contribuye activamente al seguimiento del Business Plan, entregando información clave para mantener el portafolio dentro del apetito y tolerancia al riesgo definidos.🧠 Principales funcionesMonitorear el desempeño del portafolio, identificando tendencias, desviaciones y señales tempranas de deterioro Analizar transiciones de clientes y variables de comportamiento, considerando indicadores macroeconómicos. Identificar causas del deterioro del portafolio y explicar variaciones relevantes en resultados y métricas. Proponer acciones de mitigación y alertas tempranas, apoyando la toma de decisiones del negocio. Evaluar concentraciones y riesgos de exposición, velando por una adecuada diversificación. Preparar reportes ejecutivos y dashboards para seguimiento de riesgo y Business Plan. Mantener y contribuir a metodologías de apetito y tolerancia al riesgo, concentración y sobreendeudamiento. 🧩 RequisitosFormación Ingeniería Comercial, Ingeniería Civil Industrial, Economía, Estadística, Matemáticas o carreras afines. Experiencia 2 a 3 años en áreas de riesgo, portafolio, análisis, BI o cobranzas. Herramientas R Studio, Python, herramientas BI y SQL nivel básico/intermedio. Conocimientos técnicos métricas y metodologías de análisis de riesgo de portafolio, segmentación de clientes y nociones de modelos de comportamiento. ✨¡Postula y súmate al equipo!

medtechrec is partnered with a growing, privately held global medical device organisation specialising in sports medicine to recruit a Business Development Manager to support continued expansion across Chile.The organisation operates internationally and combines strong commercial growth with a collaborative, people-led culture. This role sits at the centre of the regional commercial strategy and plays a critical part in supporting clinicians, partners, and internal stakeholders.Position OverviewThe Business Development Managert acts as the key link between the companys product portfolio, its commercial partners, and the clinical community. As the regional subject-matter expert, the role combines deep product knowledge, market intelligence, and partner enablement to ensure solutions are clearly understood, effectively positioned, and confidently adopted.Working closely with the Business Development Manager, the Product Specialist supports regional sales strategy through technical expertise, pricing insight, and strong relationship management.Key ResponsibilitiesProduct Expertise & Market InsightServe as the regional product expert, maintaining in-depth knowledge of product features, clinical applications, and supporting evidence. Monitor competitive activity and market trends to support pricing strategy and commercial decision-making. Provide data-driven insights to the Business Development Manager on pricing opportunities and challenges. Partner & Customer EnablementSupport distributor partners in developing and executing effective go-to-market strategies. Deliver structured product training and technical presentations to partners, clinicians, and internal teams. Act as the first point of contact for complex clinical or technical product enquiries. Relationship ManagementBuild and maintain strong, trust-based relationships with distributors, partners, and end-user clinicians. Represent the organisation professionally in the field, acting as a consistent brand ambassador. Sales & Strategic SupportCollaborate closely with the Business Development Manager to support regional sales planning and execution. Provide technical and clinical support throughout the sales cycle to progress commercial opportunities. Prepare technical documentation and support responses for tenders. Cross-Functional CollaborationWork closely with Marketing to support the development of sales tools, campaigns, and educational materials. Partner with Product Management to relay structured field insights that inform future product development. Ensure effective communication of market and customer feedback to internal stakeholders. Candidate ProfileBachelors degree in Business, Life Sciences, Engineering, or a related discipline. Minimum of 3 years experience in a Product Specialist, clinical support, or similar role within sports medicine. Strong ability to communicate complex technical or clinical information clearly and persuasively. Analytical mindset with the ability to translate market data into actionable insights. Proactive, organised, and comfortable operating autonomously in a field-based role. Fluent in Spanish; English proficiency is a strong advantage. Whats on OfferCompetitive salary with performance-based bonus. Opportunity to play a pivotal role in a high-growth international medical device organisation. Supportive, collaborative working environment. Clear scope for professional development and long-term career progression.

location: Las Condes, Santiago Metropolitan Region, Chile
job_description: We help the world run betterAt SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed.There are currently two open positions available for this role.Position SummaryA Technical Quality Manager is responsible for defining and orchestrating the portfolio of services to SAP Premium Engagement Customers. As trusted advisor on-site with the customer you have a crucial role in supporting the setup and execution of the customer digital transformation roadmap with SAP services. Based upon your SAP track record you have both the functional and/or technical experience and coordination skills to orchestrate SAP global teams and specialists to create tangible business outcomes for our customers. The senior Technical Quality Manager effectively combines the roles of a portfolio manager (identifying and prioritizing Premium Engagement Services with customers), and service delivery manager (ensuring Premium Engagement Services are delivered as contracted).As a TQM you are accountable for the day to day execution of the SAP MaxAttention portfolio & service plan, methods and tools, and best practices across all systems & landscape environments and platforms. You are responsible for executing on the customer s value-based, digital transformation roadmap, making full use of innovation services, orchestrating the prototyping of business scenarios, fostering a platform for ideation and innovation, and capturing the value of SAP MaxAttention. You will develop and demonstrate a premium level of understanding of the customer s strategic business goals, business processes, landscape, industry norms and all available and late breaking technology as well as newly developed SAP MaxAttention services. As a technology and business aficionado, you guide your customers on the proper adoption and deployment of predictive analytics, the internet of things, machine learning, artificial intelligence, blockchain, drones and sensors, and the positive impact these technological advancements can have on your customer, their employees and work-place safety, their end-customers and the environment.The TQM demonstrates a premium level of enthusiasm for technology enabled business advancements, clearly articulating to the customer current technology trends including public and private cloud, on-prem and hybrid technologies and trends; disruptive technologies, the industry of automation, machine learning,, visualization, PAAS, SAAS, IAAS, IOT, Industry 4. 0, cyber security, big data, artificial intelligence, blockchain, and so on.The TQM collaboratively aligns and coordinates with all other customer relevant SAP employees and keeps the regional SAP leadership team and the customer s SAP MaxAttention team aware of progress, risks, challenges, and accomplishments.SAP MaxAttention is a premium and strategic engagement with SAP s largest customers. As such, the TQM:Ensures the engagement meets the highest level of standards and excellenceDevelops and maintains a strong customer relationship and works to be recognized as a Trusted Advisor providing unbiased guidanceCollaborates with any System Integrator or Partner of the customer.Captures and reports how the engagement is driving business value to the customer, accelerating Time To Value, optimizing operations, reducing total cost of operations (TCO), and safeguarding the transformationIt Is The TQM s Responsibility ToExecute on the customer s digital transformation roadmapOrchestrate prototyping of business scenarios, and drive realization for positive outcomesEstablish powerful long-term relationships based on the LACE (Land, Adopt, Consume, Expand) operation modelBe a Trusted Advisor and "Talk Business" and Value & OutcomesSupport the technical integration of the entire solution and product set, end to end across all landscapes and platformsProvide a platform for ideation on business scenariosDrive adoption of model company and industry reference architectureFoster a culture of empathy, ideation and innovationEnsure empowerment of the customer teamRecommend adjustments to the SAP MaxAttention Service Plan as neededFacilitate and provide recommendations for technical or business process improvementsRemove complexity for the business and for end users wherever possibleIdentify top issues, define the associated action plan & drive the resolution processCoordinate involvement of all SAP resources onsite and in the back officeDeliver the right expertise to the customer at the right timeEnsure the customer receives immediate help in crisis situationsRequirementsBachelor or Masters Degree: Science, Tech

job_title: Product Manager – Portafolio de Productos Digitales
job_description: BC Tecnología es una consultora de TI que gestiona portafolio, desarrolla proyectos y ofrece outsourcing y selección de profesionales. Nos enfocamos en equipos ágiles para Infraestructura, Desarrollo de Software y Unidades de Negocio, atendiendo clientes de servicios financieros, seguros, retail y gobierno. Diseñamos soluciones a la medida, con énfasis en consultoría, formación de equipos, desarrollo de proyectos y servicios de soporte y administración IT. Nuestro objetivo es impulsar la transformación digital de nuestros clientes mediante enfoques colaborativos, metodologías ágiles y un fuerte foco en el cliente. FuncionesSer arquitecto estratégico del portafolio de productos digitales, garantizando entrega de valor medible al negocio y al cliente final.Conectar la visión de necesidades reales de los usuarios con los objetivos comerciales de cada país, transformando insights en decisiones de producto.Dirigir la estrategia, evolución y performance del ecosistema de productos, asegurando que cada desarrollo responda a hipótesis de valor validadas.Trabajar alineado con las capacidades core de la organización y contribuir al roadmap global de transformación digital.Más que gestionar un backlog, liderar iniciativas de impacto, priorización basada en datos y seguimiento de resultados.Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para asegurar entrega ágil, calidad y escalabilidad.Mentorizar y desarrollar equipos de producto para fomentar una cultura de valor y liderazgo. DescripciónBuscamos un Product Manager senior que actúe como arquitecto estratégico del portfolio de productos digitales bajo su dominio. Su misión es garantizar la entrega de valor medible, traduciendo necesidades de usuarios y objetivos comerciales en decisiones de producto concretas. Integrará visión regional con la estrategia global, ajustando enfoques a realidades locales sin perder consistencia. Será responsable de la evolución y performance de su ecosistema de productos, manteniendo alineación con las capacidades centrales de la organización y apoyando el roadmap de transformación digital a nivel regional.Habilidades clave: Pensamiento sistémico, storytelling ejecutivo, liderazgo orientado a datos, Gestión de la Complejidad Regional y Mentoría de líderes de producto que prioricen el valor sobre las características. Requisitos deseablesExperiencia comprobable en gestión de portafolio y productos digitales en contextos regionales; capacidad para traducir datos y métricas en decisiones estratégicas; experiencia en escenarios de multi-país y en transformación digital; habilidades de comunicación influyentes a nivel ejecutivo y capacidad de mentoría de equipos. BeneficiosEn BC Tecnología promovemos un ambiente de trabajo colaborativo que valora el compromiso y el aprendizaje constante. Nuestra cultura se orienta al crecimiento profesional a través de la integración y el intercambio de conocimientos entre equipos.La modalidad híbrida que ofrecemos, ubicada en Las Condes, permite combinar la flexibilidad del trabajo remoto con la colaboración presencial, facilitando un mejor equilibrio y dinamismo laboral.Participarás en proyectos innovadores con clientes de alto nivel y sectores diversos, en un entorno que fomenta la inclusión, el respeto y el desarrollo técnico y profesional.

¿Qué esperamos de ti?En Principal estamos orgullosos de poner nuestro propósito al centro de todo lo que hacemos, motivados por la misión de fomentar un mundo en donde todas las personas tengan acceso a la seguridad financiera. Esta misión, junto con la integridad, foco en el cliente y la experiencia de más de 140 años en la industria nos ha posicionado como un referente en el mercado financiero.Buscamos un Operador Mesa de Dinero quien tendrá la misión de colaborar con el portfolio manager en la administración del portafolio de los diferentes fondos bajo administración con el fin de cumplir con los objetivos de estos, tanto de rentabilidad como de riesgo, a través de una correcta ejecución de las inversiones, análisis y propuestas. Desarrollar y mantener sistemas y planillas, a fin de contribuir con la operatividad de las unidades de negocio según los estándares definidos por la Compañía.¿Qué esperamos de ti?Asegurar operatoria dentro de los estándares de la compañía, en búsqueda de una excelencia operativa.Desarrollo, mantención y seguimiento de bases de datos y sistemas que permitan un manejo prolijo de los fondos.Mantenerse informado de noticias y eventos relevantes de los mercados financieros globales.Mantener contacto fluido con las contrapartes.Participar de reuniones del manejo de los fondos.¿Qué buscamos?Profesionales de Ingeniería Comercial, Civil Industrial o afines.0-1 años de experiencia en cargos similares.Manejo avanzado en Excel e inglés.Contar con conocimientos generales en Python.Contar con acreditación CMV en Operador (deseable).Nuestra Cultura de TrabajoSomos conscientes que un adecuado equilibrio es la mejor forma de tener equipos exitosos y empoderados, por lo cual adoptamos un enfoque de flexibilidad que se adapta a tus necesidades y de tu equipo con base en una cultura de confianza. Nuestro compromiso es entregarte los recursos y oportunidades que necesitas para ser exitoso.¿Qué podemos ofrecerte? ¡Flexibilidad! Modalidad de trabajo hibrida 4x1.Más tiempo para ti: Tenemos una jornada menor a la legal, de 39,5 hrs. SemanalesLos viernes trabajamos hasta las 14:00 hrs. y además podrás tener hasta 10 días de vacaciones adicionales al año.Seguro complementario de salud y dental.Espacios de trabajo colaborativos para que te sientas cómodo asistiendo a la oficina.Oportunidades y herramientas para impulsar tu desarrollo profesional.APV 1+1Experiencia PrincipalEn Principal, valoramos el balance tanto a nivel personal como profesional. Juntos, estamos imaginando un futuro orientado a darle propósito a los servicios financieros, y eso comienza contigo. Nuestro éxito depende de las experiencias y talentos únicos de nuestros colaboradores. Apoyándolos de la misma manera que a nuestros clientes: con ofertas de beneficios integrales y competitivas diseñadas para proteger su bienestar físico, financiero y social.Empleador Inclusivo Tenemos un compromiso con la igualdad de oportunidades, por lo que todos los solicitantes cualificados recibirán consideración para el empleo sin distinción de raza, color, religión, sexo, orientación sexual, identidad de género, origen nacional, o capacidades físicas.

location: Las Condes, Santiago Metropolitan Region, Chile

job_description: Do you want to make a difference in the world and contribute to a sustainable future?About The Job Strategic Impact: Lead FLSmidth Excel Foundry & Machine spare parts growth and market penetration in Chile, directly shaping business results. Leadership & Influence: Drive cross-functional collaboration, engaging both internal and external stakeholders to secure best outcomes. Customer Value Creation: Represent both technical and commercial excellence to build long-term partnerships with key mining customers.About FLS And Excel Foundry & MachineFLS is a global leader in providing sustainable productivity to the mining industry. With a strong focus on innovation, safety, and customer value creation, we deliver high-quality equipment, services, and aftermarket solutions that support the entire mine lifecycle. We are committed to building long-term partnerships and driving operational excellence for our customers worldwide.Excel Foundry & Machine was founded in 1929 in Pekin, Illinois, the company first produced small brass bushings before expanding in the 1970s into bronze wear parts for the mining industry. Known for quality, service, and fast delivery, Excel became a trusted source for replacement parts in mining and aggregates.Today, Excel is recognized as a full-line provider of high-performance crusher replacement parts, mill lift inserts, undercarriage components for shovels, and a growing line of replacement parts for large-scale open pit mining equipment. In 2006, the company was acquired by FLSmidth A/S and continues to operate as part of the group, doing business as Excel, while carrying forward its legacy of innovation, quality, and reliability.https://www.excelfoundry.com/enAbout The Department The Service Business Line - Spare Parts - Excel Foundry & Machine focuses on delivering high-performance spare parts, aftermarket solutions and services tailored to our mining customers' most demanding operational needs. By combining technical expertise with customer-focused strategies, we ensure uptime, reliability, and long-term value creation across Chile.The sales representative reports operationally to Head of SAMER Sales of FLSmidth Excel Foundry & Machine.Your Responsibilities Arrange sales calls and meet with customers to sell our Excel HPP portfolio. Act as the primary customer contact, building and maintaining strong relationships known for excellent customer service. Develop and implement short-to-mid-term planning strategies to protect, grow and diversify our relationship with customers. Provides sales technical assistance and product training. Collaborate with internal teams to maximize sales potential and draft proposals. Visit customer sites to consult on equipment operations, replacement requirements and pending scope of work. Maintain comprehensive account plans for key accounts. Manage expense accounts to keep costs in line with budgeted target while adhering to defined budget guidelines. Complete regular entries and updates in CRMWhat You Bring B.S. degree in Engineering or equivalent experience and 10+ years of experience selling in the mining industry Must have mining equipment industry experience with the ability to read technical drawings, manuals, and parts list. Proactive nature with effective communication & presentation skills and strong problem-solving ability. Eager to travel, 50% of the time should be spent conducting visits to our customers’ mining and aggregate plants. Travel is primarily by company-provided car. Antofagasta is highly preferred but will consider other locations based on distance from major mine sites. The territory of coverage will be select accounts within Chile. The primary focus of this position will be supporting key accounts in core mining territories as well as adjacent industries in the states above. Direct engagement and knowledge of these markets within this region as well as other industrial plants is a plus.What We OfferInclude all benefits relevant to the job. It can be specific benefits like health insurance or highlighting features about FLSmidth – E.g. as an equal opportunity employer.Other Details; Closing practicalities- deadline for application etc. List details such as contact person etc. Please make sure to have application deadline as one of the details. We are committed to building long-term partnerships and driving operational excellence for our customers worldwide.Excel Foundry & Machine was founded in 1929 in Pekin, Illinois, the company first produced small brass bushings before expanding in the 1970s into bronze wear parts for the mining industry. Known for quality, service, and fast delivery, Excel became a trusted source for replacement parts in mining and aggregates.Today, Excel is recognized as a full-line provider of high-performance crusher replacement parts, mill lift inserts, undercarriage components for shovels, and a growing line of replacement parts for large-scale open pit mining equipment. In 2006, the company was acquired by FLSmidth A/S and continues to operate as part of the group, doing business as Excel, while carrying forward its legacy of innovation, quality, and reliability.https://www.excelfoundry.com/enAbout The Department The Service Business Line - Spare Parts - Excel Foundry & Machine focuses on delivering high-performance spare parts, aftermarket solutions and services tailored to our mining customers' most demanding operational needs. Antofagasta is highly preferred but will consider other locations based on distance from major mine sites. The territory of coverage will be select accounts within Chile. The primary focus of this position will be supporting key accounts in core mining territories as well as adjacent industries in the states above. Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.The company is founder led, profitable and growing.We are hiring a Partner Sales Manager to be a key contributing team member within the growing IHV alliances organization. Large OEM or Independent Hardware Vendor (IHV) brands - Dell, HPE, IBM, Lenovo, Ericsson, Cisco, Fujitsu and many more - are major partners for Canonical. These companies build software-defined solutions to capitalize on global open source mandates and associated macro trends. Canonical's flagship product, Ubuntu, and its broader open source portfolio are key ingredients for these partners to realize their aspirations. Canonical represents the best platform for rapid open source innovation.Reporting to the Head of IHV Alliances, the Partner Sales Manager will work closely with IHV and Canonical teams in various geographies to enable IHV teams, generate and transact opportunities, drive a Canonical agenda within an IHV, and achieve targeted results. They are responsible to build trusted relationships with our partners, increase Canonical market share and attach rate, evangelize the partnership and drive business interactions from across persona - from engineer to CxO. They will often run customer workshops focused on particular initiatives at that customer, attend sales events, give public presentations and participate in executive engagements.Location: This is a remote role.The role entailsBuild strategic relationships and enable partner teams on the partnershipBuild pipeline and transact opportunities through an IHV Grow partner awareness of open source capabilities on Canonical UbuntuDemonstrate a deep understanding of the Linux and cloud software ecosystemDeliver on targets, objectives and provide a voice of the partnerTravel regularly - sometimes internationally - to drive partnerships in personAlign and support internal canonical field teams - identify, support, grow, transactExpand existing footprint with IHV customers with an aim to upsell to broader portfolioSupport and contribute to broader strategy, initiatives and key campaignsWhat we are looking for in youExperience in alliance or indirect sales management rolesAutonomous, disciplined, hands-on, get-it-done mentalityAbility to capture customer requirements, evaluate gaps, identify and create opportunitiesPassionate about Ubuntu products and missionComfortable in fast-paced and high pressure environments with measurable goalsExperience with Linux, virtualization, containers, and other cloud technologiesExcellent communication and presentation skillsTeam player with superior accountability and customer support skillsCredibility and working knowledge of IHVs - their products, go-to-market motion, and fieldExperience managing cross-functional teams and track record of operational excellenceWillingness to travel up to 4 times a year for internal eventsHands on experience with Salesforce and Google Suite a plusWhat we offer colleaguesWe consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognize outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in personPersonal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per yearAnnual compensation reviewRecognition rewardsAnnual holiday leaveMaternity and paternity leaveTeam Member Assistance Program & Wellness PlatformOpportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleaguesPriority Pass and travel upgrades for long-haul company eventsAbout CanonicalCanonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open-source projects and the platform for AI, IoT, and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence; in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since our inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future and wi

IntroducciónEmpresa de arquitectura especializada en proyectos de retail, con una operación de alto volumen y múltiples proyectos en paralelo, se encuentra en búsqueda de un/a Controller de Administración y Control Operativo, con un perfil senior, práctico y fuertemente orientado a la ejecución y control de gestión.Buscamos un profesional no financiero tradicional, sino un perfil de control y administración hands-on, capaz de involucrarse directamente en la operación diaria, asegurar el control efectivo de costos, pagos y procesos administrativos, y entregar información clara, oportuna y confiable para la toma de decisiones.El rol requiere una alta capacidad para manejar gran volumen de proyectos (cerca de 300 anuales), múltiples centros de costo y un elevado número de transacciones por proyecto. La posición actúa como un filtro crítico de control operativo, evitando errores, duplicidades de pago y desviaciones administrativas que impactan directamente en la operación.La posición trabaja de forma muy cercana con la dirección de la empresa y con el equipo contable interno y externo, tanto en Chile como en Colombia, con foco principal en la operación chilena.Habilidades BlandasFuerte orientación a la ejecución y al trabajo operativo diario.Alta tolerancia al detalle y a la revisión minuciosa de información.Capacidad real de "arremangarse" y resolver problemas directamente en la operación.Pensamiento crítico, autocrítico y ética intachable en el manejo de recursos.Habilidad para trabajar de forma transversal con equipos técnicos, contables y operativos.Comunicación clara, directa y didáctica, especialmente con perfiles no financieros.Capacidad de adaptación a entornos dinámicos, con alta carga operativa y múltiples frentes abiertos.Proactividad, iniciativa y sentido común, con vocación de servicio a clientes internos, externos y proveedores.Perfil comprometido con el negocio, cercano a la operación y alineado con la cultura de la empresa.ResponsabilidadesEjecutar y supervisar el control administrativo y operativo de múltiples proyectos en paralelo, asegurando el correcto uso de presupuestos y centros de costo.Revisar y validar de forma diaria facturas, pagos y registros administrativos, detectando y corrigiendo errores, duplicidades o inconsistencias.Asegurar la correcta y oportuna gestión de pagos a proveedores, resolviendo directamente reclamos o diferencias cuando sea necesario.Supervisar y coordinar el trabajo del equipo contable interno en Chile y del proveedor contable externo en Colombia.Consolidar información administrativa y financiera de ambas operaciones, con foco principal en Chile.Elaborar y validar reportes semanales de gestión: estado de proyectos, facturación, cuentas por cobrar, flujos de caja y control de costos.Implementar y mantener controles internos que aseguren trazabilidad, orden y confiabilidad de la información.Participar activamente en la implementación y mejora de herramientas tecnológicas de control y reportería, especialmente ERP y Power BI.Actuar como contraparte técnica frente a la dirección, entregando información clara, confiable y accionable para la toma de decisiones.Identificar oportunidades de mejora en procesos administrativos y de control, proponiendo soluciones prácticas y aplicables. (Por ejemplo: Rendición de gastos, registro de horas, control de compra de insumos de oficina, etc.)Relacionarse activamente con proveedores clave para comprender y controlar el flujo completo de trabajo, desde la compra hasta el cierre administrativo y financiero de cada proyecto.Liderar el cierre administrativo y financiero de proyectos y centros de costo, evaluando resultados consolidados, desviaciones presupuestarias y márgenes, y presentando dicha información a las distintas áreas de la empresa.Analizar los resultados de la operación y colaborar en la definición de criterios de evaluación de desempeño, asegurando coherencia entre resultados del negocio, métricas de control y eventuales esquemas de incentivos o bonos.Buscar, proponer y liderar la implementación de soluciones tecnológicas o de sistemas que permitan integrar la información de resultados financieros, control de gestión, evaluación de desempeño y otorgamiento de bonos mejorando la trazabilidad y calidad de la información para la toma de decisiones.Habilidades TécnicasDominio avanzado de Excel, orientado al control, análisis y validación de grandes volúmenes de información.Experiencia comprobada en el uso de ERP, idealmente Softland, con foco en control operativo y administrativo.Experiencia en implementación o uso de Power BI u otras herramientas de reportería para información en tiempo real.Capacidad de análisis financiero aplicado a la operación (costos, márgenes, desvíos), más que a finanzas corporativas tradicionales.Conocimientos contables suficientes para validar información financiera, sin requerir un enfoque financiero sofisticado o bancarioRequisitosExperiencia previa en cargos de control de gestión, administració

We’re seeking a bold, strategic finance leader who thrives in complexity and excels at business partnering. In this high-impact role, you’ll serve as a Financial Controller to the Market Organization, helping to steer financial levers and performance.You’ll be working closely with senior executives to shape decisions, drive integration, and ensure long-term value creation. If you're looking for a growth platform within Hilti that could catapult you to future leadership roles, this is it.What You'll DoBe confident in financial accounting topics including month end closing, Profit & Loss Statement (P&L), Balance Sheet, statutory accounting, audit.Be the champion of the P&L (Profit & Loss statement) and bring transparency on the value creation of the various functions.Develop and drive a wide range of financial analysis focused on business trends and evaluation of performance, projections and reports.Be analytical and conceptually savvy and enjoy working with a big volume of data.Establish clear, consistent reporting processes and reporting landscape for your organization and be the champion for the tools. Ensure timely and accurate monthly deployment.Shape & execute full performance management cycle including orchestration of all relevant stakeholders.Recognize opportunities with financial impact across the organization and propose options to exploit further, able to assess initiatives with end to end view.Collaborate with different functions to understand the business model and its financial impacts, to support the management in decision making.Communicate and present effectively to Cross-Functional Team members.High self-development and drive to develop himself/herself and others.What You’ll BringDemonstrated knowledge in Accounting, Controlling, Reporting or Financial Advisory in a financial, consultancy or auditing role within a multinational organizationEffective communication skills, including ability to simplify a financial message to non-finance stakeholders and adapt communication to different levels in the organization Strong business planning, financial planning (forecasting/budgeting) and analysis skillsExperience in financial planning, modelling and analysis and reporting software preferred (e.g. Excel, Access, Tagetik, SAP Analytics Cloud, SQL, Tableau, Power BI, etc)Demonstrated resilience to face challenging and critical situations in a constructive and decisive manner, dealing with internal business partnersDemonstrated commitment to business improvement and be able to drive change to achieve resultsProject management experience and stakeholders management in new initiatives to reinforce continuous improvementWhat’s In It for YouBenefitsWe really value our people, and we've worked hard to develop a reward package that reflects this. Some of our benefits include:Annual Bonus according to resultsPrivate Health Insurance Paid leave to work on charitable projectsHybrid scheme of work with 50% of flexibility Employee resource groups focused diversity and sustainability topicsAmong othersWhen applying to a position at Hilti, you can expect a people-centric recruitment process where every application is reviewed by a human (automated notifications are triggered by our recruiting team). Personalized feedback isn’t provided for applications that don’t reach hiring manager interview stage, the overall process—including evaluations and interviews—typically takes 3 to 6 weeks, with most applicants hearing back within 2 to 3 weeks. For tips and more information, you’re encouraged to explore Hilti’s career FAQ section.Why HiltiHilti is a global leader in construction innovation, with more than 34,000 team members across 120 countries. Guided by our purpose, Making Construction Better, we’re driven to keep learning, growing, and finding new ways to make a lasting impact. Here, you’ll be empowered to use your strengths, work with a global and inclusive team, and take on meaningful challenges. At Hilti, you’ll have the chance to make your ideas, achievements, and growth real through purpose, passion, and teamwork.Commitment to InclusionAt Hilti, inclusion is a key focus in how we work, lead, and grow together. We are committed to embracing diversity of thought and creating an environment that is inclusive of everyone, everywhere. We continuously strive to ensure every voice is valued and every team member feels empowered to contribute. By building on this foundation, we strengthen our teams, our innovation, and our impact, making construction better together.

job_title: Controller de Operación e Inventarios E-commerce Fashions Park
job_description: 📦 ¡ En Fashion's Park S.A. buscamos un/a Controller de Operación e Inventarios E-commerce! 🔍¿Eres un apasionado de la eficiencia logística y el control de procesos? En nuestra Gerencia de E-Commerce buscamos a un perfil metódico y estratégico para asegurar que nuestra operación vuele. 🚀🎯 Tu MisiónSerás el guardián de la integridad del stock y la eficiencia operativa. Tu objetivo es auditar y optimizar la gestión de nuestra bodega externa y el inventario, garantizando que cada pedido llegue a tiempo y que nuestro stock sea 100% confiable.🛠️ ¿Cuáles serán tus desafíos?Auditoría 3PL Supervisar los KPIs críticos del operador logístico (Lead Time, Picking Accuracy y eficiencia de espacio). 📈Control de Inventario Proactivo Liderar inventarios cíclicos, analizar la rotación (Curva ABC) y gestionar mermas o saldos. 📉Acelerador de Ventas (Time-to-Market)Resolución de Incidencias Gestionar discrepancias de SKUs, problemas de sistemas y asegurar el abastecimiento de insumos (packaging, etiquetas). 📦Sincronización Logística Trabajar codo a codo con el Product Manager para priorizar la recepción de productos clave y cubrir la demanda. 🤝Gestión Marketplace Coordinar la logística específica para nuestros distintos sellers.💼 Detalles del CargoUbicación Casa Matriz, Conchalí. 🏢Reporta a Jefe de E-commerce.Área Comercial E-Commerce.✨ ¿Qué perfil buscamos?Experiencia sólida en control de inventarios y gestión de bodegas (WMS).Capacidad analítica avanzada para evaluar rotación y quiebres.Experiencia trabajando con operadores logísticos externos (3PL).Alguien detallista, resolutivo y orientado a la mejora continua de procesos.¿Estás listo para tomar el control de nuestra operación? ¡Postula ahora! 💥Fashion’s Park es una cadena de retail especializada en vestuario para toda la familia con cerca de 3.000 colaboradores distribuidos entre Chile y China.Con más de 35 años de trayectoria y 100 tiendas desde Arica hasta Punta Arenas, nos hemos consolidado como una marca cercana y accesible.Nuestro propósito es "Hacer felices a quienes nos eligen", entregando una experiencia de compra única y memorable.Si quieres estar al tanto de nuestras novedades, tendencias de moda y promociones, síguenos en redes sociales📷 Instagram @Fashions_park📘 Facebook @tiendasfashionspark🐦 X @Fashions_Park_▶️ YouTube @TiendasFashionsPark💼 LinkedIn Fashion’s Park
